params.name: Crazy Horse Memorial
params.description:
On Sep 7, 1900 AD, the American archaeologist Edgar dug up a clay tablet while excavating in Iraq.
The Princeton Clay Tablet is an artifact of the ancient Babylonian civilization, dating back to 1900 BC, and is currently housed in the Columbia University Library in the US. The tablet is inscribed with a mysterious matrix of numbers in four columns and fifteen rows, initially interpreted as related to commercial bookkeeping, but later confirmed to contain the principles of the Pythagorean theo …
